classDescription: A swaggering raider, duelist, and sea-scoundrel who wins through mobility, pressure, dirty tricks, and presence.
roleIdentity: Dexterity/Charisma martial controller who mixes movement, debuffs, leadership, and opportunistic burst damage.

pirate-sea-legs.name: Sea Legs
pirate-sea-legs.summary: You ignore difficult terrain from water, ships, rigging, and unstable surfaces. You can't be knocked prone by vehicle or vessel movement, and you have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed.
pirate-sea-legs.description:
Years at sea have given you preternatural balance and footing. You gain the following benefits:

- Difficult terrain caused by water, mud, sand, ship decks, rigging, gangplanks, or other unstable surfaces doesn't cost you extra movement.
- You can't be knocked Prone by the motion of a vehicle or vessel.
- You have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed.
- You have advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks made to keep your balance.

pirate-swagger-dice.name: Swagger Dice
pirate-swagger-dice.summary: You have a pool of Swagger Dice — your class resource — that power your Dirty Fighting tricks and other features. Start with 2 uses (d6); die size and count increase as you level.
pirate-swagger-dice.description:
You have a pool of Swagger Dice that represent your nerve, bravado, and timing in a fight. You expend these dice to fuel Dirty Fighting tricks, Boarding Action, Marked Quarry, and other class features.

You regain all expended Swagger Dice when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

**Swagger Die size by level:**
- d6: levels 1–4
- d8: levels 5–12
- d10: levels 13–16
- d12: levels 17–20

**Swagger Dice count by level:**
- 2 dice: levels 1–2
- 3 dice: levels 3–6
- 4 dice: levels 7–12
- 5 dice: levels 13–16
- 6 dice: levels 17–20

pirate-dirty-fighting.name: Dirty Fighting
pirate-dirty-fighting.summary: When you hit with a weapon attack, expend one Swagger Die to add its result to damage and apply a dirty trick: Blind, Cheap Shot, Shove Off, or Unfoot.
pirate-dirty-fighting.description:
When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one Swagger Die to perform a dirty trick as part of that attack. Roll the die and add the result to your damage roll, then choose one of the following tricks:

**Blind Them.** The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier) or be Blinded until the end of its next turn.

**Cheap Shot.** The target's speed is reduced by 15 feet until the end of its next turn.

**Shove Off.** You push the target up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice.

**Unfoot.** The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (same DC) or fall Prone.

You gain additional trick options as you reach higher levels: 3 tricks at 3rd level, 4 at 6th, 5 at 9th, 6 at 14th, and 7 at 20th.

pirate-cunning-footwork.name: Cunning Footwork
pirate-cunning-footwork.summary: You can take the Disengage or Dodge action as a Bonus Action. You can also move through the space of any creature that is your size or larger.
pirate-cunning-footwork.description:
Starting at 2nd level, you can take the Disengage or Dodge action as a Bonus Action on each of your turns.

Additionally, you can move through the space of any creature that is your size or larger. Moving through a hostile creature's space doesn't trigger opportunity attacks from that creature.

pirate-boarding-action.name: Boarding Action
pirate-boarding-action.summary: When you move at least 10 ft toward a creature and hit it with an attack, expend one Swagger Die to add its result to damage and move up to 10 additional feet without provoking opportunity attacks.
pirate-boarding-action.description:
Starting at 2nd level, when you move at least 10 feet in a straight line toward a creature and then hit it with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one Swagger Die as part of that attack.

Roll the Swagger Die and add the result to your attack's damage roll. You can then immediately move up to 10 additional feet in any direction without provoking opportunity attacks.

This movement occurs after the attack resolves.

pirate-pirate-calling-subclass.name: Pirate Calling
pirate-pirate-calling-subclass.summary: At 3rd level, choose a Pirate Calling subclass. Your calling defines your style and grants features at levels 3, 10, and 15.
pirate-pirate-calling-subclass.description:
At 3rd level, you choose the style of pirate you have become — your Pirate Calling. Your calling grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 10th and 15th level.

Your calling might be the cunning **Duelist** (blade-focused, high mobility, parry and riposte), the commanding **Buccaneer Captain** (crew leadership, morale, and momentum), the shadowy **Sea Ghost** (infiltration, ambush, and ghostly presence), or the terrifying **Terror of the Deep** (fear, overwhelming force, and no survivors).

pirate-marked-quarry.name: Marked Quarry
pirate-marked-quarry.summary: As a Bonus Action, mark one creature you can see within 60 ft. Once per turn when you deal damage to your Quarry, deal extra damage equal to your Swagger Die. Lasts 1 minute. Charisma modifier uses per Long Rest.
pirate-marked-quarry.description:
Starting at 3rd level, as a Bonus Action, you mark one creature you can see within 60 feet as your Quarry. The mark lasts for 1 minute.

While the mark lasts:
- Once per turn when you deal damage to your Quarry with a weapon attack, you deal extra damage equal to one roll of your Swagger Die.
- You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to locate your Quarry.
- You know the direction to your Quarry as long as it is within 1 mile and on the same plane.

The mark ends early if you mark a different target, fall Unconscious, or finish a Long Rest.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-1.name: Ability Score Improvement
pirate-ability-score-improvement-1.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-1.description:
When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
pirate-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-2.description:
When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-3.name: Ability Score Improvement
pirate-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-3.description:
When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

pirate-ability-score-improvement-4.name: Ability Score Improvement
pirate-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
pirate-ability-score-improvement-4.description:
When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

pirate-extra-attack.name: Extra Attack
pirate-extra-attack.summary: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
pirate-extra-attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

pirate-swagger-dice-d8.name: Swagger Dice (d8)
pirate-swagger-dice-d8.summary: At 5th level, your Swagger Dice improve from d6 to d8, increasing the potency of all features that use them.
pirate-swagger-dice-d8.description: At 5th level, your Swagger Dice improve from d6s to d8s. All features that expend or roll a Swagger Die — including Dirty Fighting, Boarding Action, Marked Quarry, and similar abilities — now use the larger die.

pirate-no-quarter.name: No Quarter
pirate-no-quarter.summary: Once per turn, when you hit a creature that is Frightened, Prone, Restrained, or below half its Hit Point maximum, roll one Swagger Die and add the result to your damage.
pirate-no-quarter.description:
Starting at 6th level, you show no mercy to weakened foes. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, if that creature is Frightened, Prone, Restrained, Incapacitated, or has fewer Hit Points remaining than half its Hit Point maximum, you can roll one Swagger Die and add the result to your damage roll.

This additional damage doesn't cost a Swagger Die — it is a free roll.

pirate-uncanny-balance.name: Uncanny Balance
pirate-uncanny-balance.summary: You can't be knocked Prone unless you choose to be. You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws to avoid falling, and difficult terrain never costs you extra movement.
pirate-uncanny-balance.description:
Beginning at 7th level, your footing is nearly impossible to disrupt. You gain the following benefits:

- You can't be knocked Prone against your will. If an effect would knock you Prone, you can choose to remain standing.
- You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws to avoid falling.
- Difficult terrain of any kind never costs you extra movement.

pirate-menacing-reputation.name: Menacing Reputation
pirate-menacing-reputation.summary: You gain Expertise in Intimidation. When you roll initiative, hostile creatures that can see you and fail a Wisdom save (DC 8 + proficiency + Charisma mod) are Frightened of you until the end of their first turn.
pirate-menacing-reputation.description:
By 9th level, your legend precedes you wherever you sail. You gain the following benefits:

- You gain Expertise in Intimidation (your proficiency bonus is doubled for Charisma (Intimidation) checks). If you already have Expertise, you instead gain a +2 bonus.
- When you roll initiative, each hostile creature within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be Frightened of you until the end of its first turn in combat.

pirate-subclass-feature-10.name: Pirate Calling Feature
pirate-subclass-feature-10.summary: At 10th level, your Pirate Calling grants its second feature.
pirate-subclass-feature-10.description: At 10th level, your chosen Pirate Calling grants you an additional feature. Consult your subclass for the specific benefit.

pirate-press-the-advantage.name: Press the Advantage
pirate-press-the-advantage.summary: When an ally within 30 ft scores a critical hit or reduces a creature to 0 HP, use your Reaction to move up to half your speed and make one weapon attack. Charisma modifier uses per Long Rest.
pirate-press-the-advantage.description:
Starting at 11th level, when an ally within 30 feet of you scores a critical hit or reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, you can use your Reaction to move up to half your speed (without provoking opportunity attacks) and make one weapon attack against a creature of your choice.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

pirate-swagger-dice-d10.name: Swagger Dice (d10)
pirate-swagger-dice-d10.summary: At 13th level, your Swagger Dice improve from d8 to d10, increasing the potency of all features that use them.
pirate-swagger-dice-d10.description: At 13th level, your Swagger Dice improve from d8s to d10s. All features that expend or roll a Swagger Die now use the larger die.

pirate-master-of-the-boarding-line.name: Master of the Boarding Line
pirate-master-of-the-boarding-line.summary: You move freely through enemy spaces, suffer no opportunity attacks when climbing, and can use a Bonus Action to move up to your speed vertically without falling.
pirate-master-of-the-boarding-line.description:
At 13th level, you have total command of vertical and confined spaces. You gain the following benefits:

- Moving through the space of any creature — regardless of size — doesn't provoke opportunity attacks from that creature.
- Climbing costs you no extra movement, even on difficult surfaces.
- As a Bonus Action, you can move up to your speed vertically (along walls, rigging, or rope) without falling, even without a surface to grip. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

pirate-legend-of-the-waves.name: Legend of the Waves
pirate-legend-of-the-waves.summary: You are immune to the Frightened condition. Once per turn when you take the Attack action, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.
pirate-legend-of-the-waves.description:
At 14th level, your reputation and seasoned nerves make you immune to fear. You gain the following benefits:

- You are immune to the Frightened condition.
- Once per turn when you take the Attack action, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that same action. This attack can target the same creature or a different one.

pirate-subclass-feature-15.name: Pirate Calling Feature
pirate-subclass-feature-15.summary: At 15th level, your Pirate Calling grants its final feature.
pirate-subclass-feature-15.description: At 15th level, your chosen Pirate Calling grants you its final feature. Consult your subclass for the specific benefit.

pirate-dread-volley.name: Dread Volley
pirate-dread-volley.summary: As an Action, expend up to 3 Swagger Dice to make one weapon attack per die spent. Each hit deals weapon damage plus the rolled Swagger Die. Recharges on a Short or Long Rest.
pirate-dread-volley.description:
Starting at 17th level, as an Action, you can expend up to 3 Swagger Dice and unleash a rapid barrage of attacks. Make one weapon attack for each Swagger Die you expend (up to 3 attacks), each targeting a creature within your weapon's range.

For each attack that hits, deal your normal weapon damage plus roll the expended Swagger Die and add it to the damage.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

pirate-indomitable-swagger.name: Indomitable Swagger
pirate-indomitable-swagger.summary: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can expend one Swagger Die to drop to 1 Hit Point instead. Once per Long Rest.
pirate-indomitable-swagger.description:
At 18th level, you refuse to be taken down. When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can expend one Swagger Die — no action required. If you do, you drop to 1 Hit Point instead of 0.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Additionally, you can't be frightened, charmed, or compelled to flee against your will.

pirate-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
pirate-epic-boon.summary: At 19th level, you gain an Epic Boon feat. The recommended boon for Pirates is Boon of Combat Prowess or Boon of Speed.
pirate-epic-boon.description:
At 19th level, you gain a special ability called an Epic Boon — a feat available only to characters of 19th level or higher. Choose one Epic Boon feat.

The recommended Epic Boons for Pirates include the Boon of Combat Prowess (never miss when you need it most) or the Boon of Speed (extraordinary mobility at any moment).

pirate-king-of-the-black-flag.name: King of the Black Flag
pirate-king-of-the-black-flag.summary: At 20th level, your Swagger Dice become d12s, and when you roll initiative you gain bonus Swagger Dice equal to your Charisma modifier. Once per turn, you can use a Dirty Fighting trick without expending a Swagger Die.
pirate-king-of-the-black-flag.description:
At 20th level, you become the undisputed terror of any sea, dungeon, or battlefield. You gain the following benefits:

- Your Swagger Dice improve to d12s.
- When you roll initiative, you immediately gain a number of temporary Swagger Dice equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). These bonus dice last until the end of the encounter and stack with your normal Swagger Dice pool.
- Once per turn, you can use one Dirty Fighting trick without expending a Swagger Die.
- Friendly creatures within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you have Advantage on attack rolls on their first turn of each combat.

future: Surface dirty tricks as actionable buttons in the Actions tab and map-side quick panel.
Add stronger ship-combat integrations for rigging, boarding, morale, and cannon-adjacent play.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers attack rolls, AC, and Swagger Dice save DCs
buildTips.secondaryReason: sharpens class features, social presence, and most subclass themes
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you fight in medium armor at close range and need the HP to survive trades
